coredata.dc:title: A Novel State of Charge Estimation Method for Ternary Lithium Batteries Based on System Function and Extended Kalman Filter
coredata.prism:copyright: Copyright © 2020 The Authors. Published by ESG. Published by Elsevier B.V.
coredata.prism:publisher: The Authors. Published by ESG. Published by Elsevier B.V.
coredata.dc:creator: Xin, Xiong
Wang, Shun-Li
Yu, Chun-Mei
Cong, Jiang
Coffie-Ken, James
coredata.dc:description: The Equivalent Thevenin model is establish to describe the external performance of ternary lithium ion battery base on Hybrid Pulse Power Characteristic experiment by identifying and verifying model parameters. The state of charge estimation model is built in SIMULINK using Extended Kalman Filter algorithm and system function of MATLAB. The Simulation results under the working conditions of the experimental data shows that, the maximum error of the Thevenin model is 0.08V and its average error is 0.04V. Based on the Thevenin model, the state of charge estimation result shows a better performance in accuracy and robustness compare with traditional coulomb counting method. The maximum error is less than 0.01 and the average error is 0.008 which shows the systematic process and the correctness of the estimation method in this paper.
coredata.dcterms:subject: Ternary lithium battery；State of charge；Extended Kalman Filter；System function

xocs:doc.xocs:rawtext: A Novel State of Charge Estimation Method for Ternary Lithium Batteries Based on System Function and Extended Kalman Filter Xiong Xin, Shun-Li Wang, 497420789@qq.com Chun-Mei Yu, Jiang Cong, James Coffie-Ken, School of Information Engineering, Southwest University of Science and Technology, Mianyang 621010, China; School of Information Engineering Southwest University of Science and Technology Mianyang 621010 China School of Information Engineering, Southwest University of Science and Technology, Mianyang 621010, China; The Equivalent Thevenin model is establish to describe the external performance of ternary lithium ion battery base on Hybrid Pulse Power Characteristic experiment by identifying and verifying model parameters. The state of charge estimation model is built in SIMULINK using Extended Kalman Filter algorithm and system function of MATLAB. The Simulation results under the working conditions of the experimental data shows that, the maximum error of the Thevenin model is 0.08V and its average error is 0.04V. Based on the Thevenin model, the state of charge estimation result shows a better performance in accuracy and robustness compare with traditional coulomb counting method. The maximum error is less than 0.01 and the average error is 0.008 which shows the systematic process and the correctness of the estimation method in this paper. Keywords Ternary lithium batteryï¼›State of chargeï¼›Extended Kalman Filterï¼›System function References 1. Y. Chaochun, Int J Electrochem Sci. (2018) 1131-Y. simple-head.ce:title: A Novel State of Charge Estimation Method for Ternary Lithium Batteries Based on System Function and Extended Kalman Filter

ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: School of Information Engineering, Southwest University of Science and Technology, Mianyang 621010, China;

sa:affiliation.sa:organization: Southwest University of Science and Technology

ce:affiliation.ce:source-text: School of Information Engineering, Southwest University of Science and Technology, Mianyang 621010, China;

ce:abstract-sec.ce:simple-para: The Equivalent Thevenin model is establish to describe the external performance of ternary lithium ion battery base on Hybrid Pulse Power Characteristic experiment by identifying and verifying model parameters. The state of charge estimation model is built in SIMULINK using Extended Kalman Filter algorithm and system function of MATLAB. The Simulation results under the working conditions of the experimental data shows that, the maximum error of the Thevenin model is 0.08V and its average error is 0.04V. Based on the Thevenin model, the state of charge estimation result shows a better performance in accuracy and robustness compare with traditional coulomb counting method. The maximum error is less than 0.01 and the average error is 0.008 which shows the systematic process and the correctness of the estimation method in this paper.

ce:keyword.ce:text: Ternary lithium battery；State of charge；Extended Kalman Filter；System function
